_readme: RealisticOres configuration. Each ore type is a top-level key.
1 block = 1 meter. Frequency guide: 1 / frequency ≈ structure size in blocks.
These are sparsity values from 0.0 to 1.0.
0.0 = present everywhere | 0.5 = present in ~50% of area | 1.0 = never present.
regionThreshold 0.80 → ~20% of world is inside a coal basin.
Coal basins exist on virtually every continent; 20% is realistic and playable.
Within a basin: shallow_tertiary is the accessible early-game seam (~25% of basin columns).
Deep seams are rarer within basins (~10-15% of basin columns) and are late-game resources.
presenceFrequency ~0.001 → ~1000-block seam diameter (≈ 1 km). Realistic for major seams.
Threshold compensation keeps total coal coverage similar to vanilla while making individual
seams much larger and separated by realistic coal-free gaps.
Y >= deepRankY  →  Bituminous coal  →  coal_ore dominant (coreThresholdShallow)

geology._regionThreshold_note: 0.80 = coal in ~20% of world. Realistic (coal widespread globally) and playable.
geology._gasThreshold_note: 0.95 = gas in ~5% of seam volume. Rare but present, as in real coal mines.

_notes: Deepest seam. Late-game resource equivalent to anthracite in the deepslate zone.
Rare within basins (high thresholds). Large seam blobs (~1050 block diameter) but sparse.
Realistic: deep primary seams in the Appalachian Basin extend hundreds of km but are
patchy within that range due to erosion, faulting, and depositional variation.

size._presenceThreshold_note: 0.72 → ~28% of basin columns hit this seam. Deep seam = rare.
size._secondaryThreshold_note: 0.62 → further ~38% reduction. Creates internal gaps and lenses.

_notes: Mid-depth seam. Standard bituminous coal zone. Splitting enabled to simulate the
Pittsburgh coal bed style where a sandstone channel splits the seam into two benches.
Moderately rare within basins (threshold 0.69). Larger blobs than shallow seam but
interrupted by secondary noise more aggressively.

split._threshold_note: 0.62 → ~38% of seam area splits into two benches.

_notes: The primary early-game coal source. Deliberately more accessible than deep seams.
Lower presence/secondary thresholds = more continuous seam coverage within a basin.
Net coverage within basin: ~42% * 55% ≈ 23% of basin columns hit this seam.
Total world coverage: ~20% basin * 23% = ~4.6% of world XZ columns at this Y range.
This means: a player IN a basin finds coal within ~50-100 blocks. A player OUTSIDE
a basin may need to travel 500-1000 blocks — realistic, but not punishing.
centerY fixed at 58, amplitude 8 → range Y=50-66, stays below surface.

depth._amplitude_note: Amplitude 8 → Y range 50-66. Always sub-surface, never exposed.

size._presenceThreshold_note: 0.58 → ~42% of basin columns. Accessible early-game seam.
size._secondaryThreshold_note: 0.45 → ~55% pass. Less fragmentation = more findable by player.

composition._coreShallow_note: Only 12% coal_block in shallow zone. Bituminous coal is mostly ore.
